skill.name: angular-20-standalone-component
skill.description: Create Angular 20 standalone components with Signals, input() and output(), modern control flow, inject() dependency injection, and OnPush change detection.
skill.summary: Build Angular 20 standalone components using current framework patterns and layered application boundaries.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from markdown formatting and Angular example code. No shell execution, weak cryptographic implementation, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Modern Angular projects need consistent standalone components that avoid outdated patterns. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create components with Signals, inject(), and current template syntax.
actual_capabilities: Provides a standalone Angular component template using Signals and OnPush change detection.
Shows input() and output() usage instead of legacy decorator patterns.
Demonstrates modern @if, @for, and @switch template control flow.
Guides dependency injection through inject() instead of constructor injection.
Includes examples for smart components, presentational components, subscriptions, and reactive forms.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not an executable Angular schematic.
It assumes project services, shared imports, and UI components already exist.
It does not cover routing, unit tests, end-to-end tests, or deployment setup.
Some referenced project guide files are not included in this packaged skill.

use_cases.title: Create feature components
use_cases.description: Generate a new Angular 20 component that follows standalone architecture, reactive state, and project service boundaries.

use_cases.title: Modernize component patterns
use_cases.description: Replace decorator inputs, constructor injection, and legacy template directives with current Angular syntax.

use_cases.title: Standardize team output
use_cases.description: Give AI assistants a shared component pattern for consistent code review and implementation results.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Angular 20 standalone component for displaying a list of tasks. Use Signals, input(), output(), @for, and OnPush.

prompt_templates.title: Add loading and error states
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an Angular 20 standalone component that loads items from a service and shows loading, error, empty, and success states.

prompt_templates.title: Create a form component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standalone Angular 20 form component with ReactiveFormsModule, validation, submit output, and modern control flow.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor legacy component
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Angular component to use standalone imports, Signals, input()/output(), inject(), takeUntilDestroyed(), and @if/@for syntax.

output_examples.input: Ask for a task list component with service loading and item updates.
output_examples.output: A component plan with standalone imports, signal-based state, loading and error states, service methods, and event outputs.

output_examples.input: Ask to modernize a component that uses @Input, @Output, and *ngFor.
output_examples.output: A refactoring response that replaces legacy patterns with input(), output(), @for, inject(), and OnPush change detection.

output_examples.input: Ask for a task form with validation and submit handling.
output_examples.output: A form-oriented response that includes reactive validation, disabled submit behavior, output emission, and form reset behavior.

best_practices: Provide the component purpose, data inputs, events, and service names before asking for generation.
Ask the assistant to preserve project imports and naming conventions from nearby components.
Review generated code for real project types, service contracts, and UI library compatibility.
anti_patterns: Do not request NgModule-based components when the project requires standalone components.
Do not mix decorator inputs with input() functions in the same new component.
Do not place repository or business logic directly inside generated presentation components.

faq.question: Does this skill generate Angular 20 standalone components?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides AI assistants to create standalone components with current Angular 20 patterns.

faq.question: Does it use Angular Signals?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends signal(), computed(), and effect() for component state and derived values.

faq.question: Does it support legacy Angular syntax?
faq.answer: No. It explicitly discourages NgModules, @Input decorators, @Output decorators, *ngIf, and *ngFor.

faq.question: Can it create form components?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a reactive form example with validators, submit handling, and output events.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown guide and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
